title: THE USE OF TRICHLOROACETIC ACID FOR THE EXTRACTION OF PHYTIN OF FOODSTUFFS.
author: M. L. SARMA
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: A method for the estimation of the phytin content of foodstuffs is described, in which trichloroacetic add is used for the extraction of phytin instead of the usual HCl extraction. It is suggested that the modification introduced in this paper is more suitable for deter­mining the distribution of the acid-soluble phosphorus compounds in foodstuffs.
steps_raw: A method for the estimation of the phytin content of foodstuffs is described, in which trichloroacetic add is used for the extraction of phytin instead of the usual HCl extraction. It is suggested that the modification introduced in this paper is more suitable for deter­mining the distribution of the acid-soluble phosphorus compounds in foodstuffs.
